title: QA: Quality-gate runner shells timeout+command via bash -c without separation (guardrails.rs)
description:
## Severity
Critical
## Dimension
security
## Location
src/guardrails.rs:492-541
## Violation
`run_shell_command_sync` builds and executes a shell string by
interpolating two attacker-influenced fields:
```rust
let actual_command = if timeout_seconds > 0 {
    format!("timeout {timeout_seconds} {command}")
} else {
    command.to_string()
};
...
Command::new("bash").args(["-c", &actual_command])
```
`command` comes from `QualityCheck.command` (config-loaded). Any check
authored to call user-supplied tooling now runs through a layered shell
with zero escaping or allowlisting. There is no audit log of what was
actually executed beyond the bare `info!(name = %check.name, "Running
quality gate")`; stderr is captured but not surfaced to a security log
channel.
## Mandated refactor
Replace `format!("timeout {ts} {command}")` with explicit `argv`:
build `Command::new("timeout").args([&ts.to_string(), "bash", "-c", command])`
when shell is unavoidable — but require quality-gate authors to declare
`program: String` + `args: Vec<String>` and drop `bash -c` entirely. Emit
a structured audit-log event with full argv for every quality-gate
invocation before spawning.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
